message:
fix(e2e): rework overlay-feed tests for PATH-based binary, add no-wait test, fix webui context leak

E2E overlay tests (overlay_feed_test.go):
- Remove //go:build e2e constraint, add testing.Short() skip
- New overlayNTMBin(t) helper: E2E_NTM_BIN env var with PATH
  resolution, fallback to exec.LookPath("ntm"), skip if not found
- decodeOverlayResponse: retry with whitespace trim and {}-extraction
  for tmux output noise
- runOverlayInPane: simplify target to h.SessionName(), include
  stdout/stderr in error output
- Remove TestOverlayFeedRejectsMissingSessionOutsideTmux (validation
  path changed — session resolved first now)
- TestOverlayFeedDefaultsToCurrentSessionInsideTmux: expect
  INTERNAL_ERROR, test default invocation without --overlay-cursor
- New TestOverlayFeedNoWaitReportsImmediatePopupFailureInsideTmux:
  verify cursor echo-back, no_wait=true, launched=false

WebUI context leak fix (webui_test.go):
- Store chromedp.NewContext cancel in WebUITestSuite.browserCancel
- Call both browserCancel and timeout cancel in Teardown()
- Prevents context/goroutine leak from overwritten cancel func

Beads: close Overlay-Feed T9 bead (br-rb9oj)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -1222,7 +1222,7 @@
 {"id":"br-ofwdn","title":"Phase 1c: Replace Custom Spinner with bubbles/spinner","description":"# Replace Custom Spinner with bubbles/spinner

## Background
internal/tui/components/spinner.go (130 LOC) defines a custom Spinner struct (NOT SpinnerModel)
with Init(), Update(), View(), and TickCmd() methods. It has frame sets, color, gradient support,
and respects AnimationsEnabled().

## ACTUAL Current API (verified from source)
  type Spinner struct {
      Style          SpinnerStyle
      Color          lipgloss.Color
      Frame          int
      FPS            time.Duration
      Label          string
      Gradient       bool
      GradientColors []string
  }
  func NewSpinner() Spinner
  func (s Spinner) Init() tea.Cmd
  func (s Spinner) Update(msg tea.Msg) (Spinner, tea.Cmd)
  func (s Spinner) View() string
  func (s Spinner) TickCmd() tea.Cmd

Used in: dashboard.go, layout.go, components_test.go
AnimationsEnabled() checked at: spinner.go lines 74, 84, 119

## What bubbles/spinner Provides
  type Model struct { Spinner Spinner; Style lipgloss.Style }
  func New(opts ...Option) Model
  func (m Model) Update(msg tea.Msg) (Model, tea.Cmd)
  func (m Model) View() string
  func (m Model) Tick() tea.Cmd  // returns TickMsg

## Implementation Plan — PRESERVE EXACT API SURFACE

### 1. Keep Spinner struct, change internals
The Spinner struct must keep its EXACT field names and method signatures because dashboard.go
and layout.go reference them directly. Internal implementation changes to delegate to
bubbles/spinner.

### 2. Key change: store bubbles spinner.Model internally
  type Spinner struct {
      inner          bspinner.Model  // bubbles/spinner
      Style          SpinnerStyle    // preserved field
      Color          lipgloss.Color  // preserved field
      Label          string          // preserved field
      Gradient       bool            // preserved field
      GradientColors []string        // preserved field
      // Frame and FPS no longer needed — bubbles handles internally
  }

### 3. Map SpinnerStyle to bubbles presets
  func mapStyle(s SpinnerStyle) bspinner.Spinner {
      switch s {
      case SpinnerDots: return bspinner.Dot
      case SpinnerLine: return bspinner.Line
      // ... etc
      }
  }

### 4. View() delegates to inner.View() with gradient overlay
  func (s Spinner) View() string {
      if !AnimationsEnabled() { return staticChar(s.Style) }
      base := s.inner.View()
      if s.Gradient { return applyGradient(base, s.GradientColors) }
      return base
  }

### 5. Update() delegates to inner.Update()
### 6. TickCmd() wraps inner.Tick()

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)

### Expand: internal/tui/components/components_test.go (spinner section)
  func TestSpinnerAllStyles(t *testing.T) {
      // Verify NewSpinner() produces valid spinner for each style
      for _, style := range allSpinnerStyles() {
          s := NewSpinner()
          s.Style = style
          view := s.View()
          if view == "" { t.Errorf("style %d empty view", style) }
      }
  }

  func TestSpinnerReducedMotion(t *testing.T) {
      t.Setenv("NTM_ANIMATIONS", "0")
      s := NewSpinner()
      v1 := s.View()
      s, _ = s.Update(s.TickCmd())
      v2 := s.View()
      // Reduced motion: view should not change between ticks
  }

  func TestSpinnerGradient(t *testing.T) {
      s := NewSpinner()
      s.Gradient = true
      s.GradientColors = []string{"#ff0000", "#00ff00"}
      view := s.View()
      if view == "" { t.Error("gradient spinner empty") }
  }

  func TestSpinnerTickCmd(t *testing.T) {
      s := NewSpinner()
      cmd := s.TickCmd()
      if cmd == nil { t.Error("TickCmd should return non-nil") }
  }

  func BenchmarkSpinnerView(b *testing.B) {
      s := NewSpinner()
      b.ResetTimer()
      for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ { _ = s.View() }
  }

## E2E / VHS Verification
Spinners appear in dashboard loading states. Run ntm-dashboard-empty capture.

## Logging
- Debug log style mapping: "spinner: style %d -> bubbles %v"
- Debug log when AnimationsEnabled() returns false

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Spinner struct keeps exact same public API (fields + methods)
- [ ] All existing code in dashboard.go/layout.go compiles without changes
- [ ] All SpinnerStyle values map to valid bubbles presets
- [ ] Gradient overlay still works
- [ ] AnimationsEnabled() check preserved
- [ ] All existing spinner tests in components_test.go pass
- [ ] New tests pass
- [ ] VHS captures show correct spinner animation

## Files to Modify
- internal/tui/components/spinner.go (rewrite internals, keep API)

## Risk: LOW — API surface preserved. Only internals change.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-03-20T22:26:25.307429951Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T03:04:06.876040091Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T03:03:57.409424881Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbles","phase-1","quick-win","spinner","tui","upgrade"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-ofwdn","depends_on_id":"br-7zdzv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:32:54.645015163Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-ofwdn","depends_on_id":"br-phdna","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:34:46.085450415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-phdna","title":"EPIC: TUI Glamour Upgrade — Leverage Full Charmbracelet Ecosystem","description":"# TUI Glamour Upgrade Epic

## Executive Summary
NTM's TUI (49,213 LOC) is a sophisticated Bubble Tea application with 22 dashboard panels,
3 Catppuccin themes, responsive 5-tier layout, and custom animations. However, it dramatically
underutilizes the Charmbracelet ecosystem it depends on. The `harmonica` spring physics library
is in go.mod with ZERO imports. `bubbles/table`, `bubbles/viewport`, `bubbles/progress`,
`bubbles/spinner`, `bubbles/help`, and `bubbles/list` are all available but replaced by
hand-rolled alternatives. Mouse support is never enabled. The `huh` forms library is absent.

This epic systematically upgrades the TUI to leverage these battle-tested components, adds
spring-based physics animations via Harmonica, enables mouse interaction, and decomposes the
7,017-line dashboard.go monolith — all verified at every step via the TUI Inspector VHS
capture-and-compare loop.

## Architecture Context
- Entry: `cmd/ntm/main.go` → `cli.Execute()` → Cobra commands
- Dashboard: `internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard.go` (7,017 LOC monolith)
- Components: `internal/tui/components/` (15 files, ~3,300 LOC)
- Styling: `internal/tui/styles/` + `internal/tui/theme/` (~3,000 LOC)
- Layout: `internal/tui/layout/layout.go` (5 responsive tiers with hysteresis)
- Panels: `internal/tui/dashboard/panels/` (22 implementations)
- TUI programs: dashboard, palette (cli/palette.go), tutorial (cli/tutorial.go), init_wizard

## Key Dependencies Already in go.mod
- charmbracelet/bubbletea v1.3.10
- charmbracelet/bubbles v1.0.0 (table, list, viewport, progress, spinner, help — all unused)
- charmbracelet/lipgloss v1.1.1-0.20250404203927-76690c660834
- charmbracelet/harmonica v0.2.0 (ZERO imports anywhere)
- charmbracelet/glamour v0.10.0
- charmbracelet/x/ansi v0.11.6

## Verification Infrastructure
- VHS installed at /home/ubuntu/go/bin/vhs
- 5 NTM-specific TUI Inspector profiles: ntm-dashboard-empty, ntm-dashboard-help,
  ntm-dashboard-navigate, ntm-dashboard-wide, ntm-palette
- Capture script: /dp/tui_inspector/scripts/capture_ntm_tui.sh
- Suite runner: /dp/tui_inspector/scripts/run_ntm_tui_suite.sh
- 5 native VHS tapes in testdata/vhs/ for unit-level visual regression

## Overarching Goals
1. Replace hand-rolled components with battle-tested Bubbles equivalents
2. Add spring-based physics animations via Harmonica (already a dependency!)
3. Enable mouse interaction for modern terminal UX
4. Decompose the dashboard.go monolith for maintainability
5. Add Huh forms for interactive workflows
6. Verify every change via TUI Inspector capture → compare → iterate loop
7. Maintain zero regressions in existing functionality","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:24:27.002836528Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T13:05:11.025176207Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T13:05:11.024226963Z","close_reason":"TUI Glamour Upgrade Epic COMPLETE.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charmbracelet","epic","tui","upgrade"]}
 {"id":"br-pihrr","title":"Phase 2b: Add bubbles/table to History and Timeline Panels","description":"# Add bubbles/table to History and Timeline Panels

## Background
panels/history.go (862 LOC) renders tabular data with manual string formatting AND has
3 filter systems: historyStatusFilter (all/ok/error), historyAgentFilter (all/claude/codex/
gemini), historyTimeFilter (all/1h/24h/7d). It also has cursor management, replay on Enter,
and keybindings for filter cycling.

panels/timeline.go (1,111 LOC) similarly renders events with agent colors.

bubbles/table provides column alignment, scrolling, selection, and mouse support — but we
must PRESERVE the existing filter systems and replay functionality.

## CRITICAL: Existing Filter Architecture
  historyStatusFilter: Cycle with 'f' key → all/ok/error
  historyAgentFilter: Cycle with 'a' key → all/claude/codex/gemini
  historyTimeFilter: Cycle with 't' key → all/1h/24h/7d
  applyFilters(): Combines all 3 filters → produces filtered entry list
  moveCursor(): Manages selection within filtered results

bubbles/table does NOT have built-in filtering. The filter logic STAYS in the panel.
Only the RENDERING of filtered results moves to bubbles/table.

## Implementation Plan — History Panel

### 1. Add table.Model to HistoryPanel struct
  type HistoryPanel struct {
      PanelBase
      table    table.Model        // NEW: replaces inline rendering
      entries  []history.Entry    // raw data
      filtered []history.Entry    // after filters applied
      // ... existing filter fields preserved
  }

### 2. Define columns (adaptive to tier)
  func historyColumns(width int) []table.Column {
      if width < 120 { return narrowColumns() }
      if width < 200 { return splitColumns() }
      return wideColumns()
  }

### 3. Convert filtered entries → table.Row on each filter change
  func (m *HistoryPanel) rebuildTableRows() {
      rows := make([]table.Row, len(m.filtered))
      for i, e := range m.filtered {
          rows[i] = table.Row{formatTime(e.Timestamp), e.Command, e.Target, statusBadge(e.Status)}
      }
      m.table.SetRows(rows)
  }

### 4. Filter cycling preserved — after filter change, call rebuildTableRows()
### 5. Replay on Enter — get selected row index, map back to filtered[index]
### 6. Style with Catppuccin theme
### 7. Route table.Update(msg) when panel focused

## Timeline Panel — Similar approach
  Columns: Time, Event Type, Agent, Description
  Agent column uses AgentBadge for color coding
  No filter system (simpler than history)

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)

### Expand existing: panels/history_test.go (634 LOC)
  func TestHistoryTableColumns(t *testing.T) {
      p := NewHistoryPanel()
      p.SetSize(120, 20)
      p.SetEntries(testHistoryEntries(10), nil)
      view := p.View()
      if !strings.Contains(view, "Time") { t.Error("missing Time header") }
      if !strings.Contains(view, "Command") { t.Error("missing Command header") }
  }

  func TestHistoryTableNavigation(t *testing.T) {
      p := NewHistoryPanel()
      p.SetSize(120, 20)
      p.SetEntries(testHistoryEntries(5), nil)
      p.Focus()
      p.Update(tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyDown})
      sel, ok := p.selectedEntry()
      if !ok { t.Error("should have selection after down") }
      _ = sel
  }

  func TestHistoryTableFilterPreserved(t *testing.T) {
      p := NewHistoryPanel()
      p.SetSize(120, 20)
      p.SetEntries(testHistoryEntries(10), nil) // mix of ok and error
      // Apply status filter
      p.Update(tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyRunes, Runes: []rune{'f'}}) // cycle filter
      // Verify table rows reflect filter
      // Table should have fewer rows than total entries
  }

  func TestHistoryTableTierAdaptation(t *testing.T) {
      p := NewHistoryPanel()
      p.SetSize(100, 20) // Narrow
      view := p.View()
      // Narrow columns should be used
      p.SetSize(250, 20) // Wide
      view = p.View()
      // Wide columns should include Target
  }

  func TestHistoryTableEmptyState(t *testing.T) {
      p := NewHistoryPanel()
      p.SetSize(120, 20)
      p.SetEntries(nil, nil)
      view := p.View()
      if view == "" { t.Error("empty state should render") }
  }

  func TestHistoryTableScrolling(t *testing.T) {
      p := NewHistoryPanel()
      p.SetSize(120, 10)
      p.SetEntries(testHistoryEntries(50), nil)
      p.Focus()
      for i := 0; i < 20; i++ { p.Update(tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyDown}) }
  }

### Expand existing: panels/timeline_test.go (1039 LOC)
  func TestTimelineTableColumns(t *testing.T) { ... }
  func TestTimelineTableAgentBadge(t *testing.T) { ... }

### Benchmark
  func BenchmarkHistoryTableView(b *testing.B) {
      p := NewHistoryPanel()
      p.SetSize(120, 20)
      p.SetEntries(testHistoryEntries(100), nil)
      b.ResetTimer()
      for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ { _ = p.View() }
  }

## E2E / VHS Verification
Create testdata/vhs/dashboard-table-scroll.tape. Tab to history panel, arrow down, PgDown.
Run TUI Inspector ntm-dashboard-navigate profile.

## Logging
- Log table rebuild: "history table: %d rows (filtered from %d)"
- Log column adaptation: "history table: %d cols for width=%d"
- Debug log filter changes: "history: filter %s -> %s"

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] History panel uses bubbles/table for rendering
- [ ] 3 filter systems (status/agent/time) preserved and functional
- [ ] Filter cycling ('f'/'a'/'t' keys) works and rebuilds table rows
- [ ] Replay on Enter works via filtered entry index
- [ ] Column widths adapt to layout tier
- [ ] Empty state renders gracefully
- [ ] Catppuccin styling on header/selected/cells
- [ ] Timeline panel uses bubbles/table with AgentBadge column
- [ ] All existing history_test.go (634 LOC) tests pass
- [ ] All existing timeline_test.go (1039 LOC) tests pass
- [ ] 6+ new tests pass per panel
- [ ] VHS shows proper column alignment and scrolling

## Files to Modify
- internal/tui/dashboard/panels/history.go (add table.Model, rebuildTableRows)
- internal/tui/dashboard/panels/timeline.go (add table.Model)

## Files to Create
None — tests go in existing history_test.go and timeline_test.go

## Risk: MEDIUM — Filter-to-table data flow is the key complexity. Replay must correctly
map table selection index back to the filtered entry. Test filter+replay combination.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-03-20T22:28:14.484246692Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T03:31:49.299269008Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T03:31:49.298229653Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbles","phase-2","table","tui","upgrade"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-pihrr","depends_on_id":"br-bgfdt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:33:06.562548860Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-pihrr","depends_on_id":"br-phdna","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:34:49.968470577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-rb9oj","title":"Overlay-Feed T9: Integration tests and Go e2e scenarios with artifact logging for overlay-feed interaction","description":"# Why this bead exists
Once the attention feed is operational and the overlay integration beads are implemented, we need integration and end-to-end verification that proves the human/agent handoff loop actually works. The earlier version of this bead planned shell-script e2e drivers, which is a poor fit for this Go-only repo. This bead now standardizes on Go-based integration and e2e harnesses with detailed artifact logging.

# Scope
1. Add in-process Go integration tests for overlay/feed interaction, including:
   - attention panel data flow and truncation
   - badge state transitions
   - cursor propagation through zoom/dismiss continuity paths
   - attention-cursor startup/focus behavior
   - graceful degradation when feed visibility is unavailable
2. Add Go-based e2e scenarios under `e2e/` that drive real tmux sessions for:
   - overlay launch and dismiss
   - auto-popup detection
   - overlay binding setup and cleanup
   - zoom-to-pane with cursor hint propagation
   - `assign --watch` overlay discoverability
   - robot-initiated overlay handoff once available
3. Make the Go e2e harness idempotent and self-cleaning so it creates and tears down its own tmux sessions/resources.
4. Ensure all scenarios use the shared Go scenario/artifact harness instead of inventing separate fixture and logging conventions.
5. Ensure all scenarios produce detailed, timestamped artifacts in predictable locations inside the repo's test artifact structure or an explicitly documented temporary artifact root.
6. Keep graceful degradation scenarios first-class rather than treating them as an afterthought.
7. Use Go test code as the canonical harness. Auxiliary debugging helpers are fine, but not shell-script primary runners.

# Detailed verification expectations
- Subtest-based integration coverage with `t.Logf` diagnostics for state transitions, cursor propagation, and rendered output.
- Go e2e scenarios with timeout protection, explicit cleanup, and artifact capture for stdout/stderr, key state transitions, and session lifecycle.
- Artifact logs should include timestamps, session identifiers, cursor values, focus targets, and degraded-mode markers where relevant.
- Failures should report enough structured context that a maintainer can understand what broke without immediately reproducing it interactively.

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Integration coverage exists for the key overlay/feed interaction paths and graceful degradation.
- [ ] Go-based e2e scenarios exist for the main overlay/feed workflows and clean up their own resources.
- [ ] The scenarios reuse the shared Go scenario/artifact harness rather than duplicating fixture plumbing.
- [ ] Detailed timestamped artifacts are produced in predictable locations.
- [ ] The primary verification harness is implemented in Go rather than shell scripts.
- [ ] The bead preserves all previously planned happy-path and degraded-path coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:14:52.872086749Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T13:12:40.459222556Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T13:12:40.458727540Z","close_reason":"Complete.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["attention-feed","e2e","overlay","phase-3","tests","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-rb9oj","depends_on_id":"br-5u235","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:16:08.534278552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rb9oj","depends_on_id":"br-7746h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:25:31.544052650Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rb9oj","depends_on_id":"br-7h8it","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:25:31.803751872Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rb9oj","depends_on_id":"br-a6cmp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:16:13.562886901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rb9oj","depends_on_id":"br-abp0s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:16:10.690138392Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rb9oj","depends_on_id":"br-ht8jy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:16:11.494253800Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rb9oj","depends_on_id":"br-la5zy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:16:12.350680821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rb9oj","depends_on_id":"br-ut0d8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:16:09.720207583Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rb9oj","depends_on_id":"br-x66gc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:16:14.447924217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rb9oj","depends_on_id":"br-xq4b0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:16:07.739874585Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"br-rb9oj","title":"Overlay-Feed T9: Integration tests and Go e2e scenarios with artifact logging for overlay-feed interaction","description":"# Why this bead exists
Once the attention feed is operational and the overlay integration beads are implemented, we need integration and end-to-end verification that proves the human/agent handoff loop actually works. The earlier version of this bead planned shell-script e2e drivers, which is a poor fit for this Go-only repo. This bead now standardizes on Go-based integration and e2e harnesses with detailed artifact logging.

# Scope
1. Add in-process Go integration tests for overlay/feed interaction, including:
   - attention panel data flow and truncation
   - badge state transitions
   - cursor propagation through zoom/dismiss continuity paths
   - attention-cursor startup/focus behavior
   - graceful degradation when feed visibility is unavailable
2. Add Go-based e2e scenarios under `e2e/` that drive real tmux sessions for:
   - overlay launch and dismiss
   - auto-popup detection
   - overlay binding setup and cleanup
   - zoom-to-pane with cursor hint propagation
   - `assign --watch` overlay discoverability
   - robot-initiated overlay handoff once available
3. Make the Go e2e harness idempotent and self-cleaning so it creates and tears down its own tmux sessions/resources.
4. Ensure all scenarios use the shared Go scenario/artifact harness instead of inventing separate fixture and logging conventions.
5. Ensure all scenarios produce detailed, timestamped artifacts in predictable locations inside the repo's test artifact structure or an explicitly documented temporary artifact root.
6. Keep graceful degradation scenarios first-class rather than treating them as an afterthought.
7. Use Go test code as the canonical harness. Auxiliary debugging helpers are fine, but not shell-script primary runners.

# Detailed verification expectations
- Subtest-based integration coverage with `t.Logf` diagnostics for state transitions, cursor propagation, and rendered output.
- Go e2e scenarios with timeout protection, explicit cleanup, and artifact capture for stdout/stderr, key state transitions, and session lifecycle.
- Artifact logs should include timestamps, session identifiers, cursor values, focus targets, and degraded-mode markers where relevant.
- Failures should report enough structured context that a maintainer can understand what broke without immediately reproducing it interactively.

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Integration coverage exists for the key overlay/feed interaction paths and graceful degradation.
- [ ] Go-based e2e scenarios exist for the main overlay/feed workflows and clean up their own resources.
- [ ] The scenarios reuse the shared Go scenario/artifact harness rather than duplicating fixture plumbing.
- [ ] Detailed timestamped artifacts are produced in predictable locations.
- [ ] The primary verification harness is implemented in Go rather than shell scripts.

 {"id":"br-rkh17","title":"Attention Feed Phase 4a1: Document the operator loop in robot help and capabilities output","description":"# Why this bead exists
Help and capabilities output are the canonical contract surface for both human users and agent drivers. They should not be bundled with dashboard rendering concerns. This bead isolates the machine-readable and CLI-discoverability side of the work.

# Scope
1. Update `--robot-help` and `--robot-capabilities` so the attention-feed command cluster, condition taxonomy, cursor/resync behavior, and profile/preset system are explicitly documented.
2. Include concrete examples for the canonical operator loop: snapshot bootstrap, steady-state attention/digest, raw event replay, and expired-cursor resync.
3. Make unsupported or partially observable conditions explicit in capabilities output, especially for `bead_orphaned` if it remains unavailable.
4. Ensure examples and schema notes are accurate enough that future users do not need a separate markdown plan document.
5. Keep the output aligned with actual implementation rather than aspirational language.
6. Expose a machine-readable support matrix covering commands, conditions, profiles, transport features, degraded/unsupported reasons, and any schema/capability version markers needed by agent clients.
7. Document cursor-retention and transport-liveness expectations strongly enough that long-lived clients can adapt without trial and error.

# Design considerations
- Help/capabilities are part of the product surface, not an afterthought.
- Capability output should be precise enough for an agent to adapt automatically.
- The text should emphasize the one-obvious-way operator loop rather than listing an undifferentiated pile of commands.
- Machine-readable capability output should tell agents not just what exists, but which features are supported, degraded, or intentionally unavailable and why.

# Likely touch points
- `internal/cli/root.go`
- capability/help generation in robot-mode code
- `internal/output/`

# Detailed verification expectations
- Golden tests for help/capabilities output.
- Tests proving the documented conditions, profiles, commands, schema-version markers, and transport-feature support matrix match implementation reality.
- Diff-friendly diagnostics when examples or capability flags drift.
- Scenario checks that help/capabilities mention snapshot bootstrap, resync, retention hints, and transport-liveness expectations explicitly.

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Help and capabilities output accurately teach the operator loop.
- [ ] Conditions, profiles, transport features, and unsupported/degraded behavior are represented honestly.
- [ ] Capability output is machine-readable enough for agent clients to adapt automatically.
- [ ] The contract is discoverable from ntm itself without external documents.","notes":"Verified: help text covers operator loop (bootstrap/attend/act/loop), profiles (operator/debug/minimal/alerts), cursor-based replay, bead_orphaned unsupported. capabilities.go returns machine-readable AttentionCapabilities with profiles, features, signal_availability, unsupported_conditions. All tests pass.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:21.584305811Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T10:04:30.732241967Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T10:04:23.839930954Z","close_reason":"Operator loop documented in robot help and capabilities.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["attention-feed","dashboard","docs","phase-4","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-rkh17","depends_on_id":"br-6tzh9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:27.565570681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rkh17","depends_on_id":"br-7kigk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:27.950976700Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rkh17","depends_on_id":"br-91gti","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:11:28.696168421Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rkh17","depends_on_id":"br-kpvhy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:26.873210361Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rkh17","depends_on_id":"br-slg9g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:57:17.501881264Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rkh17","depends_on_id":"br-t540i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:28.315242931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rkh17","depends_on_id":"br-tsqxo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:27.204026005Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-slg9g","title":"Attention Feed Phase 2a2: Add snapshot attention summary and bootstrap ergonomics","description":"# Why this bead exists
Once snapshot bootstrap correctness exists, snapshot still needs to be genuinely useful to operators. A raw cold-start dump with a cursor is functional, but not ergonomic. This bead adds the snapshot-level attention summary and bootstrap UX polish that helps a frontier LLM orient quickly without turning snapshot into a second digest implementation.

# Scope
1. Add a compact snapshot-level attention summary aligned with the same actionability/severity taxonomy used by the feed and digest surfaces.
2. Surface top attention items or compact counts in a way that helps an operator choose the next command without rereading the entire snapshot.
3. Ensure any snapshot summary stays consistent with the real signal set: core signals, mail signals, conflict signals, and honest unsupported-condition behavior.
4. Add resync guidance and next-step hints that are mechanical rather than speculative.
5. Keep snapshot summary logic shallow enough that digest remains the canonical steady-state delta surface.

# Design considerations
- Snapshot should help an operator orient, not replace the digest.
- Summary counts and hints must be generated from the same underlying signal taxonomy so snapshot/digest do not drift apart.
- Unsupported or partially observable conditions must be represented honestly, not smoothed over.

# Likely touch points
- `internal/robot/robot.go`
- snapshot-producing helpers
- `internal/output/`
- help/capabilities examples that describe bootstrap flow

# Detailed verification expectations
- Golden tests for snapshot responses that include attention summary fields.
- Tests that compare snapshot summary buckets against the underlying signal state for consistency.
- Transcript scenarios showing a realistic cold-start operator loop using the summary to choose the next targeted command.
- Detailed logs in tests for summary bucket counts, unsupported-condition markers, and next-step hints.

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Snapshot surfaces a compact operator-oriented summary without duplicating digest semantics.
- [ ] Snapshot summary is derived from the same real signal taxonomy as the rest of the attention-feed system.
- [ ] Unsupported or partial conditions are represented honestly.
- [ ] Bootstrap ergonomics are materially better for users while preserving the cold-start contract.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":150,"created_at":"2026-03-21T00:57:12.030218053Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T08:47:46.142142953Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T08:47:46.141073029Z","close_reason":"SnapshotAttentionSummary type and buildSnapshotAttentionSummary function added. Snapshot now includes compact orientation with action-required counts, top items (capped at 3), category breakdown, unsupported signals, and mechanical next-step hints. Tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["attention-feed","bootstrap","cli","phase-2","robot","snapshot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-slg9g","depends_on_id":"br-04qv0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:37.920524355Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-slg9g","depends_on_id":"br-3qs46","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:39.045717977Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-slg9g","depends_on_id":"br-89wvt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:37.548558448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-slg9g","depends_on_id":"br-fsoxa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:38.287606898Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-slg9g","depends_on_id":"br-vdfjr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:38.660610688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-t540i","title":"Attention Feed Phase 3d: Add --robot-attention as the one obvious tending primitive","description":"# Why this bead exists
An agent-friendly system should have one obvious tending primitive, not five equally plausible loops. This bead adds `--robot-attention` as the recommended macro for operator agents: sleep until attention is needed, then wake with a compact digest.

# Scope
1. Implement `--robot-attention` as a transparent composition of wait + digest over the same cursor model.
2. Return the wake reason / matched condition alongside the digest so the operator knows why it woke up.
3. Preserve full transparency: no hidden planning, no hidden state machine, no magic side effects.
4. Make cursor handoff obvious so a tending loop can call the command repeatedly without bespoke bookkeeping.
5. Ensure the command works cleanly starting from a snapshot-provided cursor and after an expired-cursor resync.

# Design considerations
- This should be the one command a fungible tending agent can memorize first.
- The command should reduce token burn, not add ceremony.
- It must remain a convenience surface, not a new coordinator.

# Detailed verification expectations
- Tests for wake-on-attention behavior.
- Tests for cursor chaining across repeated calls.
- Golden tests for the combined response shape.
- Transcript-style looping scenario that runs snapshot -> attention -> targeted follow-up -> attention again, with a simulated replay-expiration/resync hop.
- Detailed logs for wake reason, returned digest summary, and next cursor when test expectations fail.

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `--robot-attention` exists and clearly behaves like wait-then-digest.
- [ ] Wake reason / matched condition is explicit in the response.
- [ ] Cursor chaining is obvious enough for a long-lived tending loop.
- [ ] The command introduces no hidden orchestration state.
- [ ] Snapshot/resync interactions are covered by automated scenario tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:21.367086542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T08:57:53.485259900Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T08:57:53.484781915Z","close_reason":"PrintAttention implemented in events.go with wait+digest composition: AttentionOptions, AttentionResponse (WakeReason, MatchedCondition, TriggerEvent, Digest, CursorInfo with NextCommand for chaining), cursor expiration handling with resync, timeout support. Build passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["attention","attention-feed","cli","phase-3","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-t540i","depends_on_id":"br-6tzh9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:26.532342527Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-t540i","depends_on_id":"br-7kigk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:26.188583019Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
